title: pgx Pooling, Query Contracts, Transactions, and Cancellation · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs pgx pooling, query contracts, transactions, and cancellation from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded pgx repository and unit-of-work layer after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Create one bounded pgxpool, verify startup deliberately, observe acquire wait, and close after admission and worker shutdown.
Use parameterized typed queries, explicit columns, row closure, iteration errors, cardinality expectations, and explainable mapping.
Run every unit-of-work statement on one transaction, defer rollback safely, commit once, and preserve the original cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Pool Lifecycle · changed case · Review Feed Go Pool Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 9615 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove pgx pool, rows, one-connection transactions, cancellation, and error ownership; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create one bounded pgxpool, verify startup deliberately, observe acquire wait, and close after admission and worker shutdown.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Classify constraint, serialization, deadlock, cancellation, timeout, connection, and unknown failures without leaking SQL or secrets.

hints: For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Sql Error Policy, name the reader, publisher, revision, Go, source, tenant, URL, feed format, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, API, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Go Sql Error Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Sql Error Policy, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 5802 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove pgx pool, rows, one-connection transactions, cancellation, and error ownership; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify constraint, serialization, deadlock, cancellation, timeout, connection, and unknown failures without leaking SQL or secrets.
